title: Forgetting and Relearning: Dirichlet Decay

runtime.expected_outcome: Applying a decay factor (a := gamma*a + delta) between episodes lets old data fade, enabling the agent to track drifting dynamics.

symbols.gamma: forgetting rate (1 = no forgetting, 0 = total reset)
symbols.Delta a: new pseudo-counts from the current episode

audiences.kid: Sometimes what you learned yesterday isn't true anymore. A little forgetting keeps the agent nimble.
audiences.real: Dirichlet decay gamma < 1 turns the structure-learner into a tracker. Old data is downweighted; recent evidence matters more.
audiences.equation: a(t+1) = gamma * a(t) + new_counts. Effective memory length ~ 1/(1-gamma).
audiences.derivation: Exponential discounting on the concentration parameters is equivalent to a time-weighted likelihood. Posterior variance now floors at a finite value, allowing tracking in non-stationary environments (Sato 2001).

orchestrate.objective: Demonstrate a practical trick for tracking non-stationary dynamics via Dirichlet decay.
orchestrate.context: corridor_turns; change the world (e.g. flip a wall) between episodes and watch the agent catch up.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 7.
Sato (2001), Online model selection based on the variational Bayes.
